ddls:
CREATE TABLE "cloudera_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each Cloudera version */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the Cloudera version (e.g., CDP 7, CDH 6.x) */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the Cloudera version */,
  "end_of_support_date" TEXT /* Date when the version will no longer receive updates or support */,
  "is_latest" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if this is the latest version */,
  "deprecation_date" TEXT /* Date when the version is marked as deprecated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "components" (
  "component_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each component */,
  "component_name" TEXT /* Name of the component (e.g., 350-e, 345-e) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the component */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of the component */,
  "is_deprecated" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the component is deprecated */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date when the component was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("component_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "component_versions" (
  "component_version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each component version */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the component */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when the component version was released */,
  "is_latest" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if this is the latest version of the component */,
  "deprecation_date" TEXT /* Date when the component version is marked as deprecated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("component_version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "compatibility_status" (
  "compatibility_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each compatibility status */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Cloudera version */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component */,
  "status" TEXT /* Compatibility status (e.g., Yes, No) */,
  "status_date" TEXT /* Date when the compatibility status was last updated */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments on the compatibility status */,
  "verified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who verified the compatibility status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("compatibility_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compatibility_status_verified_by FOREIGN KEY ("verified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compatibility_status_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_compatibility_status_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES cloudera_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "releases" (
  "release_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each release */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Cloudera version */,
  "release_type" TEXT /* Type of release (e.g., major, minor, patch) */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date */,
  "release_notes" TEXT /* Release notes */,
  "build_number" TEXT /* Build number of the release */,
  "is_latest" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if this is the latest release */,
  "deprecation_date" TEXT /* Date when the release is marked as deprecated */,
  "download_link" TEXT /* URL to download the release */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("release_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "release_components" (
  "release_component_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each release component */,
  "release_id" INTEGER /* ID of the release */,
  "component_id" INTEGER /* ID of the component */,
  "version_number" TEXT /* Version number of the component in this release */,
  "is_critical" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the component is critical for the release */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments on the component in the release */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("release_component_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_release_components_component_id FOREIGN KEY ("component_id") REFERENCES components ("component_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_release_components_release_id FOREIGN KEY ("release_id") REFERENCES releases ("release_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, QA engineer, release manager) */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Cloudera version */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES cloudera_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* ID of the Cloudera version */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_message" TEXT /* Notification message */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the notification has been read by the user */,
  "sent_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who sent the notification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_sent_by FOREIGN KEY ("sent_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_version_id FOREIGN KEY ("version_id") REFERENCES cloudera_versions ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the role is a default role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the role was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user role */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "assigned_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who assigned the role */,
  "assigned_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the role was assigned */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_assigned_by FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the permission is a default permission */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the permission was created */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)
